summary: Frontend testing for JIT contextual prompts feature. Verified: (1) Login flow works with admin credentials, (2) Dashboard shows admin role correctly, (3) New Sale page loads with customer selection screen, (4) Admin products page loads with products list and Add Product button, (5) JIT component structure is correctly implemented.

minor.issue: 403 errors on some API calls when navigating between pages. Possibly related to auth token refresh. Non-blocking for JIT feature testing.

login_with_admin_role.details: Login as admin@fmcg.com / Admin@2025 works. Dashboard shows 'Admin User ADMIN' in header.

dashboard_admin_role_display.details: Dashboard loads at /dashboard. Shows TSh 5,000.00 Today's Sales, 1 Orders Today, 14 Customers, 8 Products. Admin role displayed in top-right header.

new_sale_page_cart.details: New Sale page (cart.tsx) loads at /cart with customer selection screen showing 'Select a Customer', 'Choose or add a customer to start the sale', and 'Select Customer' button.

admin_products_page.details: Admin products page at /admin/products loads successfully with: Products title (8 product(s) • 1 categories), Add Product button (green), Bulk Import button, View toggle, Search bar, Stats (8 Total Products, 0 Low Stock, 1 Categories), and 8 products listed in table view.

add_product_button_exists.details: Add Product button found in admin products page header with green styling and + icon

jit_component_structure.details: JustInTimePrompt component correctly renders Modal with: overlay, close button, icon in colored container, title, description, primary action button, optional secondary button, and skip link ('I'll do this later').

jit_prompt_types_configured.details: Three JIT prompt types implemented: first_sale_complete (checkmark-circle icon, green), offline_detected (cloud-offline icon, amber), first_product_add (barcode icon, blue)

async_storage_tracking.details: JIT prompts use AsyncStorage with key 'jit_prompts_shown' to track shown prompts. hasPromptBeenShown() and markPromptAsShown() functions correctly implemented.

jit_integration_cart.details: cart.tsx integrates JIT prompts correctly: offline_detected at line 500 (monitors offline status), first_sale_complete at line 952 (after first successful sale). JIT render at line 2334.

jit_integration_admin_products.details: admin/products.tsx integrates JIT prompts: first_product_add at lines 644-651 (triggers after first product add when products.length === 0). JIT render at lines 1724-1730.

jit_integration_mobile_products.details: Mobile products.tsx integrates JIT prompts: first_product_add at line 144 (triggers after first product add).

structure.ui_structure: Modal overlay with animated container containing: close button, icon with colored background, title, description, primary button, optional secondary button, skip link

cart_integration.first_sale_complete_trigger: Line 952 - triggers after newSaleCount === 1 with 1.5s delay

admin_products_integration.first_product_add_trigger: Lines 643-651 - triggers after first product save when products.length === 0 with 1.5s delay

critical_code_review_comments: JIT prompts implementation is complete and follows the design spec: Modal overlay with icon, title, description, primary button, and skip link
AsyncStorage persistence is correctly implemented for tracking shown prompts
All three requested prompt types (first_sale_complete, offline_detected, first_product_add) are configured and integrated
Note: first_product_add won't trigger on admin products page since there are already 8 products (only triggers when products.length === 0)
Offline warning will only show when device goes offline and offlineModeEnabled is false
context_for_next_testing_agent: JIT prompts feature is fully implemented and integrated. Three prompt types configured: first_sale_complete (triggers after first order completion), offline_detected (triggers when going offline without offline mode), first_product_add (triggers after adding first product when inventory is empty). The app has 8 existing products so first_product_add won't trigger in current state. To test first_product_add, would need to delete all products first. Offline_detected requires simulating offline conditions.
